sourceMovie.title: Yes, Madam!
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Yes, Madam! works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Accident Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Yes, Madam! clicked for you, Accident Man (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: SPL: Kill Zone
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Yes, Madam! clicked for you, SPL: Kill Zone (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Yes, Madam!?
faq.answer: Try Accident Man, SPL: Kill Zone, SPL 2: A Time for Consequences—each shares DNA with Yes, Madam! while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Yes, Madam!?
faq.answer: Yes, Madam! (1985) is best described as Action, Comedy, Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Yes, Madam! part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "In the Line of Duty Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
